chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-2913 of 2022 [Mst. Raheela Faheem vs. Province of Sindh and Others] BEFORE: JUSTICE ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON JUSTICE RIAZAT ALI SAHAR Raja Ghulam Mohiuddin Panhwar, advocate for petitioner(s) Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri, Assistant A.G. Sindh Date of hearing & decision: 13.11.2025 ORDER ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON J. - Petitioner through this constitutional petition seeks the following relief(s):- A. That this Honourable Court may be pleased to direct the respondents to prepare a separate merit list of the female candidates in Taluka male and female Latifabad District Hyderabad, as per the 15% women quota is reserved for female candidates in the mixed school category as per the teaching & non-teaching recruitment policy 2021 & 2022. B. That the Honourable Court may be pleased to direct the chairman District selection committee and its members to appoint the petitioner in UC-73 Taluka Latifabad in the mix category school on a reserved women quota as per the teaching & non-teaching recruitment policy 2021 & 2022. C. Any other relief(s) which this Honorable Court deems fit, just and proper in favour of the petitioner may be granted. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the Sindh Education and Literacy Department announced the posts of PST / JST in different newspapers under teaching and non-teaching Recruitment Policy 2021-22; petitioner applied for the post of PST from UC-73 Latifabad, Hyderabad, that the test was conducted through IBA Sukkur; Petitioner secured 54 marks but was not considered due to the unavailability of seat in her UC, despite the policy providing 15% women quota, which was ignored; hence he prayed to allow this petition. 3.

Any other relief(s) which this Honorable Court deems fit, just and proper in favour of the petitioner may be granted. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the Sindh Education and Literacy Department announced the posts of PST / JST in different newspapers under teaching and non-teaching Recruitment Policy 2021-22; petitioner applied for the post of PST from UC-73 Latifabad, Hyderabad, that the test was conducted through IBA Sukkur; Petitioner secured 54 marks but was not considered due to the unavailability of seat in her UC, despite the policy providing 15% women quota, which was ignored; hence he prayed to allow this petition. 3. Learned A.A.G, referring the comments of respondents, states that the last candidate in UC-73 was appointed with 55 marks. The recruitment policy was followed in its letter and spirit, and the petitioner did not qualify on merit. The process is now closed, vacancies filled, and thus the petition is not maintainable and may be dismissed. 4. Upon hearing the arguments of learned counsel for the parties and perusal of record, it is transpires that the petitioner secured 54 marks for the PST post from UC-73 Taluka Latifabad, whereas the last candidate appointed in the same UC secured 55 marks. It is also noted that the recruitment process has been completed, and all vacancies have been duly filled in accordance with the Sindh Education and Literacy Department’s Teaching & Non- Teaching Staff Recruitment Policy 2021-22. 5. In these circumstances, the petitioner’s request for issuance of writ of mandamus or quo warranto cannot be granted, as the recruitment process is concluded and appointments have been made on merit. Therefore, the petition is dismissed with all pending application(s). JUDGE JUDGE Karar_Hussain/PS*
